promptOrder: renderer strategy and replacement constraints last
executionModel: Keep the baseline clone fixed and replace only the selected target area.
Prefer cluster replacement over isolated slot decoration when adjacent sections form one reading system.
Do not write proposal prose that cannot be translated into executable section or cluster structure.
rendererPolicy: Use Tailwind replacement blocks as the preferred explicit surface when the page supports replacement rendering.
Avoid higher-layer wrapper reassembly in pilot replacement mode unless the page blueprint explicitly allows it.
Preserve page identity through palette, spacing discipline, and information hierarchy before adding stylistic novelty.
qualityChecks: The replacement must read as one intentional system at the chosen scope.
Primary action and primary message must remain obvious without scanning the whole page.
No helper labels or internal guide text may leak into customer-facing output.

pagePrompt.goal: Make the home page feel like a premium commerce homepage with one clear first-screen entry sequence.
avoid: equal-weight promo cards at first read
success: hero dominates first read
quickmenu supports scanning under hero
top-stage reads as one system

clusters.goal: Stage hero and quickmenu as one coordinated top-entry system.
rules: no peer split between hero and quickmenu
visual supports headline, does not compete

pagePrompt.goal: Deliver the premium commerce home identity on mobile — thumb-first navigation, hero visible above the fold without scrolling.
avoid: desktop-scale typography carried unmodified to mobile
hero pushed below fold by header elements
success: hero occupies 60–70% of first-screen height
quickmenu is reachable with one thumb
primary CTA has at least 44px touch target

clusters.goal: Hero and quickmenu adapt to mobile without losing the top-stage coordination.
rules: quickmenu collapses to 2-row grid or horizontal-scroll band
no peer-level competition between hero and quickmenu at mobile scale
headline max 3 lines at mobile scale
support slides hidden or reduced on smallest breakpoint
image fills full width without side margins
2-row icon grid with max 4 items per row
lead item spans full row width if present

pagePrompt.goal: Keep the same home identity as PC while tightening rhythm and density for tablet.
success: tablet top-stage still reads clearly
benefit and quick navigation feel balanced

clusters.goal: Adapt the top-stage system for tablet without losing the hero-led reading order.

pagePrompt.goal: Make the first screen support-path first, with guided choice and trust before depth.
success: one obvious first action
clear triage between self-help, inquiry, store/service center

clusters.goal: Stage support search and service-entry modules as one guidance cluster.
rules: proof and trust cues feel native
tabs and panels share one support tone

pagePrompt.goal: Keep service guidance readable at tablet width — search and channel entry must not overflow or become ambiguous at mid-screen scale.
avoid: 4-column grid that overflows at tablet width
hero image that overwhelms the guidance entry headline
success: search input is full-width and clearly the primary action
service tabs remain scanable in a single or two-row layout
trust cues remain concise and readable at tablet scale

clusters.goal: Adapt the service entry cluster for tablet without losing guided-choice clarity.
rules: tabs collapse to 2-row when count exceeds 5
intro section takes 2/3 max width at tablet to avoid over-wide text lines
headline max 2 lines at tablet scale
no 2-column split for intro at tablet — single column preferred
tab label stays 1 line — truncate rather than wrap
active state must be visible without hover interaction
tab row wraps to 2 rows gracefully if count > 5

pagePrompt.goal: Ensure subscription comparison remains scanable at tablet width — 2-column plan cards and a visible application CTA without scrolling.
avoid: 3-column plan grid that becomes cramped at tablet width
subscription copy truncating key value due to narrow columns
CTA pushed below the comparison section at tablet scale
success: plan differences are visible in a 2-column card layout
price and application CTA visible without scrolling at tablet width
subscription value intro stays concise

clusters.goal: Adapt subscription value + comparison + CTA cluster for tablet without losing conversion clarity.
rules: CTA remains visible in first decision band
value intro compresses to max 2 lines at tablet scale
headline max 2 lines at tablet scale
value framing stays in the first 2/3 of section width — no full-width text sprawl
price must be visible without card interaction
compare CTA sticky or pinned near top of comparison section if possible

pagePrompt.goal: Present store discovery and booking flow with calm retail trust and locality clarity.
success: store search is obvious
store-specific benefits are quickly scannable

clusters.goal: Combine store discovery, local proof, and first CTA into one retail entry cluster.
rules: store search or nearest-store CTA stays first

pagePrompt.goal: Lead with subscription value and comparison clarity, not long explanation.
success: value proposition is readable first
comparison becomes scanable before commitment

clusters.goal: Stage value summary, comparison cue, and application CTA as one conversion cluster.
rules: apply CTA always visible in first decision range

pagePrompt.goal: Make subscription benefit, payment model, and apply action clearer than standard product details.
success: benefit and payment structure are legible before deep details

clusters.goal: Treat summary, price, and apply CTA as one conversion cluster.

pagePrompt.goal: Present curation-first lifestyle commerce with warm editorial rhythm and clear browse paths.
success: editorial curation and shopping paths feel balanced

clusters.goal: Blend story-led entry and quick product discovery without flattening both.

rules: product cards remain shoppable, not decorative

pagePrompt.goal: Combine lifestyle mood and purchase clarity without letting mood obscure decision making.
avoid: story-only hero with weak buy path
success: mood and purchase path coexist without competing equally

clusters.goal: Tie gallery, key value summary, and purchase start into one lifestyle PDP cluster.

rules: mood supports value proposition, not replaces it

pagePrompt.goal: Keep browse-first behavior while clarifying recommendation and filter rhythm.
success: users can scan, filter, and compare without distraction

clusters.goal: Make category intro, filters, and first product set feel like one browse-start system.

rules: intro/banner must not outrank browse controls

pagePrompt.goal: Stage refrigerator browse with compare-first clarity and restrained premium merchandising.
success: first screen tells users how to browse and compare immediately

clusters.goal: Treat intro, filters, and first comparison-ready cards as one system.
rules: filters and compare cues remain dominant
support browse, never compete with it
key specs remain readable at scan speed

pagePrompt.goal: Make summary, pricing, and option understanding quicker than long feature reading.
success: users understand the product and buying path inside the first decision band

clusters.goal: Treat gallery, summary, and buybox as one decision cluster.

rules: price and CTA remain dominant after summary

pagePrompt.goal: Lead with premium value proposition while keeping summary and purchase action concrete.
avoid: overlong premium story before value clarity
success: premium difference is obvious before deep reading

clusters.goal: Unify premium message, proof, and buy path in one decision cluster.
rules: buy CTA stays close to price
story sharpens value, not bloats it
purchase clarity never yields to styling

pagePrompt.goal: Present storage value, size fit, and buying path with calm confidence.
success: core refrigerator value is readable before long detail sections

clusters.goal: Keep summary, size/value cues, and buybox in one decision frame.

rules: pricing and CTA remain simple and stable

pagePrompt.goal: Clarify the signature premium feature and purchase value without generic luxury noise.
success: the unique feature reads instantly and supports buying confidence

clusters.goal: Unify standout feature explanation, proof, and purchase path.

rules: feature story must not bury price/options

pagePrompt.goal: Balance premium material mood with clear summary, specs, and buy action.
avoid: summary detached from price and options
success: premium material identity and purchase clarity coexist

clusters.goal: Tie material-led value proposition to summary and purchase path in one band.
rules: buy action stays practical and visible
style tone stays restrained and credible
price and CTA remain anchored to the summary
